title: Level 1.5
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Bodoni Moda'); /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Bodoni Moda'}} {{Transparent Theme}}{{Blurbackground|file=viginette7.png}}{{Background|file=Old level 1.png}}{{Blurbackground|file=viginette7.png}}{{Background|file=Old level 1.png}}{{Alt Class|no=1.5|ext=3|env=4|ent=0}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 1.5''': "'''Lurking Danger'''"}} '''Level 1.5''' is the 2.5th level of the Backrooms, and is a sub-level between [[Level 1]] and [[Level 2]]. == Description == {{Blurimage|file=Level1.1DarkHallway.jpg|width=250px|caption=A transitional area from light to dark in the sublevel.}}{{Blurimage|file=A hallway with a fire extinguisher in level 1.png|width=250px|caption=An office-like space in the sublevel, leading to a dark section of the level.}} === Physical Characteristics === '''Level 1.5''' is a sublevel in-between [[Level 1]] and [[Level 2]]. It shows a greater connection to its parent level, however, there are several key differences. '''Level 1.5''' is composed of various building types, most of which follow its parent level's warehouse-like architecture. However, variability in design is very common. Parts of the level can appear as a clean office-like appearance with carpeted floors, unfinished with a discernible presence of plywood and fiberglass insulation, or entirely concrete. Some areas of the level are lit, while the remainder is pitch black. No lights, even those brought by wanderers from other levels, function within the dark sections of '''Level 1.5'''. The sizes of the rooms one can traverse in '''Level 1.5''' vary. In general, larger rooms tend to be darker. In many of the large spaces, carpeting or other flooring is replaced with an inch-thick layer of dirt, sprouting grass that somehow survives in an artificial environment without any active source of irrigation or solar light. In smaller rooms, certain floors may consist of plain dirt, sand, or asphalt. In most of the illuminated larger rooms, a yellowish-green light source floats randomly around the room. Touching this light source without protective gear is not recommended, as being in close proximity results in acute radiation poisoning. This light source emits bursts of Morse code, which translate to directions from the light source to another nearby light source. These directions can be extremely helpful for navigating the level. However, they do not function during the level's "nighttime" ==== Stairwells ==== Periodically within the level, rusty metal staircases will appear. These staircases are the main source of exits from the level. However, the integrity of the metal has been severely degraded by long-term wear and tear, so they are rarely safe to traverse. The safest way to descend the staircase is by standing on the edge of each step. The utmost caution, and a rope, should be utilized for any traverse of the stairwells. === Resources === Most food and drink present on this level are either laced with [[Object 12|poisonous materials]] or are inedible. Most crates in the level are full of useful supplies, excepting food. Electrical power and Wi-Fi function within illuminated zones of the level. == Mental Hazards == === Alone === No wanderer has ever reported encountering another wanderer on Level 1.5. It is believed that this level is affected by [[Phenomenon 15]]. However, a series of notes have been discovered on the level, indicating that some traces of one's passage can be left in the level. === The Persistent Drip === The persistent sound of water dripping and flowing emanates from the walls of this level. The sound comes from all directions and has no source. This leads to mental distress among wanderers, as the constant dripping can shatter one's sanity. Notably, '''The Dead Silence''' does not affect this sound. === The Dead Silence === '''The Dead Silence''' is an anomaly that can be encountered when exploring '''Level 1.5'''. All noise will suddenly stop. Any sound made by the wanderer will be muffled, as if the level had been soundproofed. It is unknown what causes this phenomenon. One's mental state will start to worsen at this point, likely due to the abnormal sound characteristics. Drinking [[Object 1|Almond Water]] while swiftly making one's way to an unaffected area by the anomaly should be one's top priority. The area affected by the anomaly can range from 1 km to 100 km. Therefore, it is important to stay moving if one finds oneself in the affected area to prevent oneself from going insane. Although the level is completely quiet during '''The Dead Silence''', a distant rumble, bang, or crash sound can be heard, although it is very rare. This event is a serious mental hazard, capable of causing loss of sanity within four to five hours. ''A scrap of paper found on the floor in '''Level 1.5'''''
I can tell that I'm nearing the end. I'm out of [[Object 1|water]], I have no food, and I can feel my sanity slipping away. This persistent drip-drip-drip of water, the occasional silence, the absolute ineffectivity of light sources, they're all ruining my mind. It's been silent for six hours now. I've set up camp in a light area. Still, I can sense... ''something'' approaching. I can hear things outside, coming for me. They're coming. I must defend myself. Let this journal entry be a warning: Run, while you still can. There is nothing here for you. Nothing but death. ''I've got it all figured out...''
== Entities == No entities are present in this level. However, it is speculated that an unknown entity kills wanderers during '''The Dead Silence''', or drives them insane. == Colonies and Outposts == Due to the nature of this level, colonies and outposts are unable to be established. == Entrances & Exits == === Entrances === * Walking through sharp turns of [[Level 1]] in the dark can lead one to a part of '''Level 1.5.''' * Finding stairs in [[Level 0]] will lead one here. * Entering a large space lacking architectural consistency in [[Level 268]] might lead one here. * Finding a rare glass door within [[Level 0.01]] will transport one here. * Touching a stop sign in [[Level 157]] has a low chance of transporting one here. === Exits === * One may stumble upon a worn and corroded stairwell that only descends. Descending downwards and entering a maintenance door will lead one to [[Level 2]]. * The safer albeit longer way to return to [[Level 1]] is to find a green metal stairwell, which leads on for several flights, and reaching the bottom. * A damp and soaked staircase may gradually transition into [[Level 4]] the more a wanderer goes down the staircase. * Noclipping into the floor during '''The Dead Silence''' will lead to [[Level 27]]. ----{{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final='''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body='''Rewrite Author:''' [[User:Phoenixskies01|Phoenixskies01]]
